title: "Demir Games Announces  Give Us A Sign , a Voice-Reactive Co-Op Psychological Horror Experience" - Games Press
snippet: 3. Demir Games Announces *Give Us A Sign*, a Voice-Reactive Co-Op Psychological Horror Experience. *Give Us A Sign* is a 1–4 player co-op psychological horror experience where players investigate haunted locations, gather evidence, and survive encounters with unseen entities. The game introduces a unique voice-reactive system that turns the player’s microphone into both a tool and a threat. The world responds to your presence. Use specialized equipment to detect entities and gather evidence while working together with your team. Customize your investigator and equipment to match your playstyle and improve your chances of survival. *Give Us A Sign* will continue to grow with free content updates, introducing new regions, more dangerous entities, and deeper layers of horror. *Give Us A Sign* is currently in active development. Players can support development by adding *Give Us A Sign* to their Steam Wishlist. These files can be freely downloaded without watermarks because they were uploaded by using **Games Press Elite**.

title: Give Me a Sign, Anna Sortino (Book Review)
snippet: Books for Kids and Grown Ups. Home > Blog > Book Reviews > Review | Give Me a Sign. # Review | Give Me a Sign. ## Summary: *Give Me a Sign*. In *Give Me a Sign*, Lilah is a teen with hearing loss but who isn’t profoundly deaf. So she has always felt caught in the middle — not deaf enough to be part of the Deaf community and not fully hearing to fit in completely there either. But when she becomes a counselor at a summer camp for deaf and blind kids, her experiences (and the boy she falls in love with) change the way the views her disability. As the summer progresses, she interacts with a wide range of characters, including a hearing social media influencer who’s studying to be sign language interpreter and makes many ally faux-pas throughout the story. *Give Me a Sign* is a beautiful exploration of Deaf culture with a nostalgic summer camp setting and sweet first love. > “*Give Me A Sign* is a really good book.
